title: Direction-Finding Methods Sorter
instructions_for_student: Drag each direction-finding method into the correct bin. Think about which methods need a clear sky and which work even when the sky is overcast.

additionalProps.title: Direction-Finding Methods
additionalProps.subtitle: Chapter 4 – Exploring Magnets
additionalProps.instructionText: Drag each direction-finding method into the correct bin. Think about which methods need a clear sky and which work even when it's overcast.

cards.label: Sun Position
cards.hint: You need to see the Sun — clouds block it!
cards.reason: Visible only when sky is clear

cards.label: Star Patterns
cards.hint: Stars are hidden behind clouds at night!

cards.label: Magnetic Compass
cards.hint: A compass uses Earth's magnetic field, not the sky!
cards.reason: Uses Earth's magnetic field — sky irrelevant

cards.label: Asking Locals
cards.hint: People can guide you in any weather!
cards.reason: Human knowledge works in any weather

cards.label: Wind Direction
cards.hint: Wind blows regardless of the sky above!
cards.reason: Wind patterns work under any sky

cards.label: Cloud Shapes
cards.hint: You need to observe cloud formations — but overcast sky blocks the pattern!
cards.reason: Requires visible sky — overcast blocks patterns

bins.label: ☀️ Works When Sky is Clear
bins.subtitle: These methods need a cloudless sky

bins.label: 🌧️ Works When Sky is Overcast
bins.subtitle: These methods work even in cloudy weather

schema.description: JSON schema for the Direction Sorting Tool

title.description: Main heading shown in the header

subtitle.description: Chapter or subject label shown above the title

instructionText.description: Instructions shown to the student inside the header banner

cards.description: Array of direction-finding method cards to sort

icon.description: Lucide icon name to render on the card

correctBin.description: Which bin this card correctly belongs to

hint.description: Short hint shown when card is dropped in the wrong bin

reason.description: One-line explanation shown when card is correctly placed

bins.description: The two target bins for sorting

subtitle.description: Short description below the bin label

color.description: Hex color for the bin accent (border, header bg)

iconEmoji.description: Floating emoji shown in empty bin

quick_reference.purpose: Interactive drag-and-drop sorting activity for direction-finding methods
key_features: Two drop bins (clear sky / overcast sky)
Correct placements lock in green with a reason
Wrong placements bounce back with a hint
Score screen on completion with percentage
Fully responsive across mobile and desktop

customisation_examples.name: Default — Chapter 4 Magnets
customisation_examples.description: Standard 6-card sort as described in the NCERT Grade 6 Science textbook

customisation_examples.name: Reduced Card Set
customisation_examples.description: Use only 3 cards for younger learners

notes: Cards with correctBin: 'clear' include Sun Position, Star Patterns, Cloud Shapes
Cards with correctBin: 'overcast' include Magnetic Compass, Asking Locals, Wind Direction
The Magnetic Compass is the key concept from NCERT Chapter 4 — it works in overcast sky because it uses Earth's magnetic field
Confetti fires only when all 6 cards are placed correctly on the first attempt
